author: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon
book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.

title: Canon XXV.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Forasmuch as certain of the metropolitans, as we have heard, neglect the flocks committed to them, and delay the ordinations of bishops the holy Synod has decided that the ordinations of bishops shall take place within three months, unless an inevitable necessity should some time require the term of delay to be prolonged.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And if he shall not do this, he shall be liable to ecclesiastical penalties, and the income of the widowed church shall be kept safe by the steward of the same Church.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Let the ordination of bishops be within three months: necessity however may make the time longer.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But if anyone shall ordain counter to this decree, he shall be liable to punishment.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The revenue shall remain with the œconomus.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The “Steward of the Church” was to “take care of the revenues of the church widowed” by the death of its bishop, who was regarded as representing Him to whom the whole Church was espoused (see Eph. v.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: So in the “order of the holy and great church” of St. Sophia, the “Great Steward” is described as “taking the oversight of the widowed church” (Goar, Eucholog., p.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: 269); so Hincmar says: “Si fuerit defunctus episcopus, ego…visitatorem ipsi viduatæ designabo ecclesiæ; “and the phrase, “viduata per mortem N. nuper episcopi” became common in the West (F.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The episcopal ring was a symbol of the same idea.

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: So at St. Chrysostom’s restoration Eudoxia claimed to have “given back the bridegroom” (Serm. post redit., iv.).

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: So Bishop Wilson told Queen Caroline that he “would not leave his wife in his old age because she was poor” (Keble’s Life of Wilson, ii., 767); and Peter Mongus, having invaded the Alexandrian see while its legitimate occupant, Timothy Salophaciolus, was alive, was expelled as an “adulterer” (Liberatus, Breviar., xviij.).

verses.book: The XXX Canons of the Holy and Fourth Synods, of Chalcedon.
verses.text: This canon is found in the Corpus Juris Canonici, Gratian’s Decretum, Pars I., Dist.
